questions.questionText: The most common cause of preventable blindness worldwide is:
questions.explanation: A neglected tropical disease caused by repeated infections with Chlamydia trachomatis.

questions.questionText: A painful, tender, red, localized swelling at the margin of the eyelid resulting from an acute staphylococcal infection of an eyelash follicle is a:
questions.explanation: An external stye is an acute, painful bacterial infection.

questions.questionText: A painless, firm, distinctly nodular swelling in the eyelid, caused by chronic granulomatous inflammation of a Meibomian gland, is a:
questions.explanation: Unlike a stye, a chalazion is sterile and slow-growing.

questions.questionText: A patient working in agriculture presents with severe eye pain, photophobia, and an ulcer on the cornea with a white, fluffy, 'feathery' border and satellite lesions. The likely cause is:
questions.explanation: Trauma with vegetative matter strongly suggests a fungal etiology.

questions.questionText: A dendritic (branching) corneal ulcer that stains brightly with fluorescein dye is pathognomonic for:
questions.explanation: Steroids are severely contraindicated as they exacerbate the replication of HSV and worsen the ulcer.

questions.questionText: The classic sign of acute angle-closure glaucoma includes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.optionC: Severe eye pain and ipsilateral headache
questions.optionD: An open, normal-appearing anterior chamber angle on gonioscopy
questions.explanation: The angle is structurally narrow/closed, blocking aqueous outflow and sharply raising Intraocular Pressure (IOP).

questions.questionText: The primary pathophysiological mechanism of primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG) is:
questions.optionB: Increased resistance to aqueous outflow through the trabecular meshwork
questions.optionC: Pupillary block pushing the iris anteriorly
questions.optionD: Excessive production of aqueous humor by the ciliary body
questions.explanation: POAG is painless and progressive, causing peripheral visual field loss ('tunnel vision').

questions.questionText: A painless, progressive, profound reduction in visual acuity characterized by an opacity of the crystalline lens is a:
questions.explanation: Cataract acts as a physical barrier to light entering the eye.

questions.questionText: The hallmark finding of proliferative diabetic retinopathy, distinguishing it from non-proliferative retinopathy, is:
questions.optionB: Neovascularization (growth of new, fragile blood vessels)
questions.explanation: Ischemia drives VEGF release, causing new, weak vessels to grow, leading to vitreous hemorrhage.

questions.questionText: A highly myopic young man presents complaining of a sudden onset of 'flashing lights' (photopsia) and multiple new 'floaters', followed by a dark shadow moving like a 'curtain' over his vision. Diagnosis?
questions.explanation: A retinal tear allows fluid to separate the neurosensory retina from the underlying epithelium.
